question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits / MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a competing taker, under the precondition that a victim has submitted a bundle tx to the mempool with a partially-consumed offer (maxUnits > 0), front-run by calling Midnight.take directly to advance consumed[maker][group] to exactly maxUnits - 1, so that consumableUnits returns 1 but the subsequent bundler take reverts with ConsumedUnits (because the attacker's tx lands first and sets consumed = maxUnits), causing the bundler to catch-and-skip every offer and revert with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that a valid offer with remaining capacity must be fillable, causing scoped impact: griefing of partial fill and DoS of the bundle? Proof idea: fuzz consumed[maker][group] in [0, maxUnits] and assert that a front-run to maxUnits - 1 always causes the bundle to revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a maker, under the precondition that their offer uses maxAssets > 0 and the bundler calls consumableUnits which invokes TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits with mulDivUp rounding, cause the computed unitsToTake to be one unit higher than what Midnight will accept (because Midnight computes buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD) and checks newConsumed <= maxAssets), so the take reverts with ConsumedAssets and the bundler silently skips the offer, violating the invariant that consumableUnits must return a value that does not cause ConsumedAssets on the subsequent take, causing scoped impact: offer undercharged / griefed after partial fill? Proof idea: fuzz (tick, maxAssets, consumed, settlementFee) and assert that consumableUnits(offer) always yields units such that take(units) does not revert with ConsumedAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that a sell offer uses maxAssets > 0 and consumableUnits calls TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits with mulDivDown rounding while Midnight computes sellerAssets = units.mulDivUp(sellerPrice, WAD), cause the computed unitsToTake to yield sellerAssets > remainingAssets so the take reverts with ConsumedAssets, causing the bundler to skip the offer and fail to reach targetUnits, violating the invariant that consumableUnits must be consistent with Midnight's asset accounting, causing scoped impact: offer griefed / bundle DoS? Proof idea: fuzz (tick, maxAssets, consumed, settlementFee) for sell offers and assert sellerAssetsToUnits(remaining) always yields units such that mulDivUp(units, sellerPrice, WAD) <= remaining.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits / MidnightBundles loop] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a maker, under the precondition that two offers in the takes[] array share the same (maker, group) with maxUnits > 0, cause the bundler to compute consumableUnits for the second offer using the pre-first-take consumed value (because the first take reverted and was caught), so both offers are attempted with the same remaining budget, and if both succeed in the same block (e.g., via multicall or two sequential bundles), the group budget is exceeded, violating the invariant that consumption group budget cannot be exceeded across offers, causing scoped impact: offer overfilled / group budget bypass? Proof idea: construct two offers with same group, make first take revert via front-run, assert second take does not exceed group maxUnits.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that a sell offer (offer.buy = false) uses maxAssets > 0 and buyerAssetsToUnits uses mulDivDown rounding while Midnight computes buyerAssets = units.mulDivUp(buyerPrice, WAD), cause the actual filledBuyerAssets to exceed targetFilledBuyerAssets by 1 wei (because ceil(floor(x/p)*p) >= x), so the loop exits with filledBuyerAssets > targetFilledBuyerAssets and the require(filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets) reverts with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that the assets-target bundle must complete when sufficient offers exist, causing scoped impact: fund freeze / bundle DoS? Proof idea: fuzz (tick, targetBuyerAssets, settlementFee) for sell offers and assert the exact-equality check is always satisfiable.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget / TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that a buy offer (offer.buy = true) uses maxAssets > 0 and sellerAssetsToUnits uses mulDivUp rounding while Midnight computes sellerAssets = units.mulDivDown(sellerPrice, WAD), cause the actual filledSellerAssets to be less than targetFilledSellerAssets by 1 wei, so the require(filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets) reverts with OutOfOffers even though the offer had sufficient capacity, violating the invariant that the assets-target bundle must complete when sufficient offers exist, causing scoped impact: bundle DoS / fund freeze? Proof idea: fuzz (tick, targetSellerAssets, settlementFee) for buy offers and assert the exact-equality check is always satisfiable.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a maker, under the precondition that their offer has maxUnits = 0 and maxAssets = 0 (both zero), cause consumableUnits to return zeroFloorSub(0, consumed) = 0 for any consumed value, so unitsToTake = 0 for every offer in the bundle, causing all takes to be no-ops (0-unit takes are allowed by Midnight), the loop completes without filling any units, and the bundle reverts with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that a valid offer must contribute to filling the target, causing scoped impact: griefing / bundle DoS? Proof idea: construct takes[] with all offers having maxUnits=0 and maxAssets=0, assert bundle always reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: Midnight.setConsumed / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as an authorized operator of the maker, under the precondition that a victim's bundle tx is in the mempool, front-run by calling Midnight.setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker) to cancel all offers in the group, so consumableUnits returns 0 for every offer in the bundle, causing all takes to be 0-unit no-ops, and the bundle reverts with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that a maker's authorized operator must not be able to grief a taker's pending bundle, causing scoped impact: griefing of partial fill / bundle DoS? Proof idea: simulate front-run setConsumed(type(uint256).max) and assert bundle reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a competing taker, under the precondition that a victim's bundle contains N offers all in the same group with maxUnits = M, partially fill the group to consumed = M - epsilon before the bundle lands, so consumableUnits returns epsilon for the first offer, the take succeeds filling epsilon units, but then consumableUnits for the second offer returns 0 (group exhausted), causing the bundle to loop through all remaining offers as 0-unit no-ops and revert with OutOfOffers if targetUnits > epsilon, violating the invariant that partial fill must not prevent subsequent offers from being attempted, causing scoped impact: offer griefed after partial fill? Proof idea: fuzz epsilon in [1, targetUnits-1] and assert bundle behavior.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that the takes[] array contains duplicate offers (same Offer struct, same group, same maker), cause the bundler to call consumableUnits twice for the same offer before any take executes (since consumableUnits is a view call), both returning the same remaining capacity, then execute both takes sequentially, with the second take seeing the updated consumed state and potentially reverting with ConsumedUnits (caught), causing the bundle to under-fill and revert with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that duplicate offers in a bundle must not cause accounting inconsistency, causing scoped impact: offer reuse / accounting desync? Proof idea: construct takes[] with two identical offers and assert fill behavior.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits / Midnight.take (maxAssets path)] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that a buy offer has maxAssets > 0 and consumed = maxAssets - 1 (one asset remaining), cause consumableUnits to call buyerAssetsToUnits(1) which returns mulDivUp(1, WAD, buyerPrice) = ceil(WAD / buyerPrice) units, but Midnight computes buyerAssets = mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD) which may equal 0 (if units < WAD / buyerPrice), so newConsumed = consumed + 0 = maxAssets - 1 < maxAssets, the take succeeds as a no-op, filledUnits += units but no assets are consumed, violating the invariant that consumed accounting must reflect actual asset transfers, causing scoped impact: offer undercharged / accounting desync? Proof idea: fuzz dust-level remaining assets and assert consumed delta matches actual buyerAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that the settlement fee changes between the touchMarket call and the take call (e.g., fee setter changes the fee in the same block via a higher-priority tx), cause buyerAssetsToUnits to compute unitsToTake using the old settlement fee while Midnight.take uses the new fee, resulting in a different buyerAssets than expected, so filledBuyerAssets != targetFilledBuyerAssets and the bundle reverts with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that the bundler's fee computation must be consistent with Midnight's fee computation, causing scoped impact: bundle DoS / fund freeze? Proof idea: simulate fee change between touchMarket and take and assert bundle behavior.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a maker with a callback, under the precondition that the offer has a callback that calls Midnight.setConsumed to advance the group consumed counter during the take execution, cause the consumed state to be modified mid-bundle so that subsequent consumableUnits calls in the same bundle return 0, causing the bundle to under-fill and revert with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that consumed state must not be modifiable during bundle execution in a way that breaks fill accounting, causing scoped impact: griefed after partial fill / bundle DoS? Proof idea: implement a malicious callback that calls setConsumed and assert bundle behavior.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a maker with a callback, under the precondition that the offer has a callback that calls Midnight.take on a second offer in the same group (advancing consumed), cause the second offer's consumableUnits (computed before the first take) to be stale, so the second take in the bundle attempts to fill more units than the group budget allows, causing the second take to revert with ConsumedUnits (caught), and the bundle to under-fill and revert with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that callback-induced consumed changes must not desync bundle accounting, causing scoped impact: griefed after partial fill? Proof idea: implement callback that takes from same group and assert bundle fill accounting.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that takes[i].units is set to a value larger than consumableUnits returns (e.g., takes[i].units = type(uint256).max), cause the min() computation to correctly cap unitsToTake at consumableUnits, but if consumableUnits returns a value that causes the take to revert (due to rounding), the bundler catches the revert and skips, causing the bundle to fail to fill targetUnits even though the offer had capacity, violating the invariant that takes[i].units as an upper bound must not interact with consumableUnits rounding to cause spurious skips, causing scoped impact: offer griefed / bundle DoS? Proof idea: fuzz takes[i].units and consumableUnits return values and assert no spurious skips.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that referralFeePct is set to WAD - 1 (maximum allowed), cause units = assets - mulDivDown(assets, WAD-1, WAD) = assets - (assets - assets/WAD) = assets/WAD (rounded down), so the actual units repaid is far less than assets, and the remaining assets (nearly all of them) are sent to the referralFeeRecipient, violating the invariant that repay must reduce debt by the correct amount relative to assets paid, causing scoped impact: undercharged repayment / maker receives far less debt reduction than expected? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct near WAD and assert units repaid is proportional to assets.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a referralFeeRecipient, under the precondition that referralFeePct is set close to WAD and filledBuyerAssets is large, cause referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) to be larger than maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets, causing the final safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets) to underflow and revert, violating the invariant that the bundler must always be able to refund the remaining tokens to msg.sender, causing scoped impact: fund freeze / bundle DoS? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct and filledBuyerAssets and assert no underflow in refund computation.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits / Midnight.consumed mapping] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that two different offers from the same maker use the same group bytes32 value but different maxUnits (which is invalid per protocol docs but not enforced on-chain), cause consumableUnits to use offer.maxUnits from the offer struct while Midnight uses consumed[maker][group] which is shared, so filling one offer advances the shared counter and causes the other offer's consumableUnits to return a value inconsistent with its own maxUnits, violating the invariant that each offer's capacity must be independently tracked, causing scoped impact: offer overfilled or reused? Proof idea: construct two offers with same group but different maxUnits and assert consumed accounting.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that an offer has maxAssets > 0 and the settlement fee is at a breakpoint boundary (e.g., exactly 7 days to maturity), cause consumableUnits to compute buyerAssetsToUnits using the fee at the boundary while Midnight.take computes the fee using the same boundary but with a different interpolation result due to integer division, causing a 1-unit discrepancy in unitsToTake, so the take reverts with ConsumedAssets (caught) and the bundle fails, violating the invariant that fee computation must be consistent between consumableUnits and take, causing scoped impact: offer griefed at fee breakpoint? Proof idea: fuzz timeToMaturity at breakpoints [0,1,7,30,90,180,360] days and assert fee consistency.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that the taker has authorized the bundler and msg.sender != taker, cause the bundler to call take with taker as the seller and address(this) as receiverIfTakerIsSeller, so loan assets flow to the bundler, but if a take reverts (caught) after a previous take already sent assets to the bundler, the bundler's internal filledSellerAssets accounting is correct but the bundler holds more assets than accounted for (from the successful take), and the final transfer sends only filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets to receiver, violating the invariant that all seller assets received by the bundler must be forwarded to the receiver, causing scoped impact: fund freeze in bundler? Proof idea: construct a bundle where some takes succeed and some revert and assert bundler balance is zero after execution.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits / Midnight.take (maxAssets path, buy offer)] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that a buy offer has maxAssets > 0 and consumed is exactly maxAssets (fully consumed), cause consumableUnits to return buyerAssetsToUnits(0) = mulDivUp(0, WAD, buyerPrice) = 0, so unitsToTake = 0, and the bundler calls take with 0 units, which is a no-op that succeeds (Midnight allows 0-unit takes), so filledUnits += 0 and the loop continues without progress, eventually reverting with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that a fully consumed offer must be skipped without wasting a take call, causing scoped impact: griefed after full consumption / gas waste? Proof idea: assert that consumableUnits = 0 causes the bundler to skip the take call entirely.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / Midnight.take] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that an offer has reduceOnly = true and the buyer has no existing debt (so buyerCreditIncrease > 0), cause Midnight.take to revert with MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased (because reduceOnly requires buyerCreditIncrease == 0 for buy offers), the bundler catches the revert and skips, but consumableUnits had computed a non-zero unitsToTake, violating the invariant that consumableUnits must not return non-zero for offers that will always revert due to reduceOnly constraints, causing scoped impact: offer griefed / bundle DoS? Proof idea: construct a reduceOnly buy offer and assert bundle behavior when buyer has no debt.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that an offer has expiry set to block.timestamp (deadline boundary), cause the offer to be valid when consumableUnits is called but expired when take is called (if the block advances or the tx is delayed), causing take to revert with OfferExpired (caught), the bundler skips the offer, and the bundle fails with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that consumableUnits must only return non-zero for offers that will not expire before take executes, causing scoped impact: offer griefed at deadline boundary? Proof idea: fuzz expiry at block.timestamp and assert bundle behavior.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget / TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that a buy offer has maxAssets > 0 and targetFilledSellerAssets - filledSellerAssets is a dust amount (e.g., 1 wei), cause sellerAssetsToUnits(1) to return mulDivUp(1, WAD, sellerPrice) which may be a large number of units (if sellerPrice is very small), causing the take to consume far more units than expected and potentially exceed maxUnits or cause other accounting issues, violating the invariant that dust-level asset targets must not cause disproportionate unit consumption, causing scoped impact: offer overfilled / accounting desync? Proof idea: fuzz dust-level targetSellerAssets and assert units consumed is proportional.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Incorrect consumed-offer accounting allowing offers to be overfilled, reused, undercharged, or griefed after partial fill'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker acting as a taker, under the precondition that takes[] contains offers from multiple different makers all sharing the same group bytes32 value, cause consumableUnits to read consumed[maker_i][group] for each maker independently (since the consumed mapping is keyed by maker), so each offer's consumableUnits is computed correctly per-maker, but the bundler's loop does not account for the fact that filling one maker's offer does not affect another maker's consumed counter, violating the invariant that group budget enforcement is per-maker not cross-maker, causing scoped impact: accounting desync if integrators assume cross-maker group enforcement? Proof idea: construct takes[] with two makers sharing a group and assert consumed accounting is independent.
